The latest announcement is out from Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras ( (PBR) ).

On January 13, 2026, Petrobras announced that its 2026 Annual General Meeting is scheduled to be held on April 16, 2026, in line with the company’s Annual Corporate Events Calendar. data.title: Arcadia Biosciences Executes Inducement Financing to Bolster Liquidity
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Arcadia Biosciences ( (RKDA) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 9, 2026, Arcadia Biosciences entered into inducement agreements with certain investors to immediately exercise previously issued preferred investment options for 808,595 shares of common stock, cutting the exercise price from $9.00 to $2.575 per share. The transaction, which closed on January 12, 2026, generated approximately $2.1 million in gross proceeds earmarked for working capital and general corporate purposes, and in exchange the company issued new unregistered preferred investment options for up to 1,617,190 shares at an exercise price of $2.325, along with additional placement agent options and related fee payments to H.C. Wainwright & Co. The deal modestly strengthens Arcadia’s near-term liquidity while introducing further potential dilution, and includes restrictions on new equity issuance and variable-rate financings for defined periods, shaping the company’s capital-raising flexibility going forward.

The most recent analyst rating on (RKDA) stock is a Hold with a $2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arcadia Biosciences stock, see the RKDA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RKDA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RKDA is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—ongoing large losses and persistent cash burn—while technicals also point to a bearish trend with the stock below key moving averages. A debt-free balance sheet and mixed corporate updates provide only limited offset, and valuation metrics are not supportive given the company’s unprofitability and lack of dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on RKDA stock, click here.

More about Arcadia Biosciences

Arcadia Biosciences, Inc. (Nasdaq: RKDA), founded in 2002 and based in Dallas, is a producer and marketer of innovative wellness products, developing high-value, healthy ingredients with roots in agricultural innovation to meet consumer demand for healthier choices.

data.title: Massive Insider Move Shakes Up Redwire’s Ownership Landscape
data.description:
New insider activity at Redwire ( (RDW) ) has taken place on January 13, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Director AE RED HOLDINGS, LLC has executed a major sale of Redwire stock, offloading 5,127,071 shares in a single transaction. The total value of this sale amounts to $55,353,876, marking a substantial change in the director’s holdings and a notable development for Redwire shareholders tracking insider activity.

Recent Updates on RDW stock

In the last day, Redwire’s stock has been in focus after a series of strategic and analyst-driven updates. The company announced it is retiring the Edge Autonomy brand and fully folding its uncrewed aerial systems and related defense technologies into the Redwire name, signaling the successful integration of the June 2025 acquisition and a clearer positioning as a multi-domain aerospace and defense technology provider. Truist raised its outlook on Redwire citing a broadly favorable 2026 backdrop for the aerospace and defense sector, driven by rising aircraft production, strong aftermarket demand, and ongoing recapitalization and geopolitical tensions that should support defense spending, while noting that careful stock selection is needed amid elevated valuations, especially for smaller defense tech names. H.C. Wainwright highlighted that current consensus estimates may underestimate the revenue potential of the combined company, pointing to recent contract wins, expectations that a government shutdown will not recur, and upside from prospective awards tied to programs such as the Golden Dome missile defense initiative and the U.S. Army’s Long-Range Reconnaissance program as reasons the shares could see material improvement in 2026. Meanwhile, KeyBanc’s initiation emphasized that Redwire’s evolution into a larger, multi-domain space and defense platform following the Edge Autonomy deal brings significantly greater scale but also shareholder dilution, tempering its stance despite the strategic benefits. Overall, recent moves in sentiment around RDW appear closely tied to the perceived success of integration, sector tailwinds, contract momentum, and the balance between growth potential and dilution risk.

Spark’s Take on RDW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RDW is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (large losses and negative cash flow). Technicals are supportive in the near term but look extended (overbought) and still below the 200-day average. Earnings-call metrics (strong revenue growth, improving adjusted EBITDA, and growing backlog) are constructive, but guidance was tempered by government shutdown timing, and the new ATM program raises dilution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on RDW stock, click here.

More about Redwire

data.title: Provident Financial Services Announces CFO Retirement Transition Plan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Provident Financial Services ( (PFS) ) has provided an update.

On January 13, 2026, Provident Bank announced the planned retirement of Thomas M. Lyons, Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer of Provident Financial Services and Provident Bank, expected to occur by June 30, 2026, or upon the appointment of his successor. Lyons, who became CFO in 2011 after joining through Provident’s 2004 merger with First Savings Bank, played a central role in expanding the bank’s assets from about $6 billion at the time of that merger to nearly $25 billion today and in strengthening its presence across New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania; he will remain in his role until a successor is named and then serve in an advisory capacity during the transition, as the bank undertakes a nationwide search that underscores the importance of maintaining continuity in its financial leadership and strategic growth trajectory.

The most recent analyst rating on (PFS) stock is a Buy with a $22.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Provident Financial Services stock, see the PFS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PFS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PFS is a Outperform.

Provident Financial Services is well-positioned with strong financial performance, robust technical indicators, and an attractive valuation. The recent earnings call highlighted record earnings and revenue, supporting a positive outlook. While there are challenges, such as revenue decline and increased competition, the company’s strengths in profitability, cash flow, and market momentum contribute to a favorable stock score.

To see Spark’s full report on PFS stock, click here.

More about Provident Financial Services

Provident Bank, founded in Jersey City in 1839, is the oldest community-focused financial institution based in New Jersey and a wholly owned subsidiary of Provident Financial Services, Inc. With $24.83 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025, the bank offers a broad range of customized financial solutions for businesses and consumers across more than 140 branches in New Jersey and parts of New York and Pennsylvania, complemented by mobile and online banking and a customer contact center. It also provides fiduciary and wealth management services through Beacon Trust Company and insurance services through Provident Protection Plus, Inc.

data.title: Concentrix Earnings Call: Cash Strength Amid Impairment
data.description:
Concentrix Corporation ((CNXC)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Concentrix Balances Strong Cash Generation With Accounting Hit and Leverage Concerns

Concentrix Corporation’s latest earnings call struck a cautiously optimistic tone, as management highlighted accelerating revenue growth, record free cash flow and rapid traction in its AI platform, while acknowledging the drag from a massive noncash goodwill impairment, still-elevated leverage, and only modest growth guidance. The company emphasized that operational execution and cash generation are improving, but investors will need to look through near-term accounting noise, restructuring headwinds and margin pressure to a fuller margin and growth recovery expected in 2026.

Solid Revenue Performance Beats Guidance, But Growth Still Modest

Concentrix delivered fourth-quarter revenue of roughly $2.55 billion, translating into 3.1% constant-currency growth and coming in ahead of prior guidance, underscoring improving operational momentum. For the full fiscal year 2025, constant-currency revenue grew 2.1%, about 60 basis points above the high end of management’s own outlook, signaling that the business is stabilizing after a tough macro period. That said, growth remains in the low single digits, and management’s broader message is that the focus is on quality of revenue and long-term durability rather than chasing volume in commoditized areas.

Margins Improve Sequentially but Remain Under Historical Levels

Non-GAAP profitability showed progress but also highlighted the work still to be done. In Q4, Concentrix generated non-GAAP operating income of $323 million with a 12.7% operating margin, a sequential improvement of 40 basis points. For the full year, non-GAAP operating income reached $1.254 billion with a 12.8% margin. However, management openly noted that these margins remain below historical norms of around 14%, reflecting both strategic investments, elevated implementation costs and a pricing environment that is tougher in lower-end services. The company is guiding to further margin improvement over 2026 as cost actions and mix shift begin to show through.

Record Cash Generation Underpins the Investment Story

The standout metric of the call was cash flow. Concentrix posted a record Q4 adjusted free cash flow of more than $287 million and full-year adjusted free cash flow of $626 million, up an impressive 32% year over year—more than $100 million higher than the prior year. This strong cash performance, despite modest revenue growth and margin compression versus history, is central to management’s argument that the business is fundamentally healthy and able to fund both growth investments and balance sheet repair. The company plans to build on this trajectory, with adjusted free cash flow guided to rise further in 2026.

Capital Returns Continue Amid Deleveraging Efforts

Concentrix used its enhanced cash generation to both return capital to shareholders and chip away at leverage. In fiscal 2025, the company returned $258 million to shareholders, including approximately $169 million of buybacks—about 3.6 million shares repurchased at an average price near $47—and roughly $89 million in dividends. At the same time, it reduced net debt by about $184 million, ending the year with $327 million in cash, total debt of $4.639 billion and net debt of $4.311 billion. Total available liquidity sits around $1.6 billion, including approximately $1.1 billion of undrawn credit capacity. Management stressed its intention to keep striking a balance between shareholder returns and ongoing deleveraging.

AI Platform IXSuite Emerges as a Key Growth Engine

The company’s AI platform, IXSuite, was a major focus and a clear bright spot. Concentrix invested roughly $25 million incrementally in the platform during the year, bringing total IX-related spend to a bit over $50 million. That investment is already generating meaningful traction: the company exited fiscal 2025 with more than $60 million in annualized AI revenue from IXSuite and reported that the platform has reached breakeven profitability. Notably, more than 40% of new business now includes company-owned technology, indicating that AI and automation capabilities are increasingly embedded in client wins and should be a structural driver of both growth and margin over time.

Upgrading Revenue Quality and Strengthening Commercial Momentum

In parallel with AI investments, Concentrix is actively reshaping its revenue mix toward more complex, higher-value work. The company reduced non-complex work from 7% to 5% of total revenue, deliberately walking away from less attractive contracts. It invested about $95 million to deepen capabilities and expand its global footprint, while moving 4% of onshore business offshore to win share and lower cost. These actions are feeding into stronger commercial metrics: the annual contract value in the pipeline exiting the year was up 6%, new wins grew 9% year over year, transformational deal values climbed 14%, cross-sell and upsell deals rose 23%, and new service area deal values jumped 37%. Importantly, 98% of the company’s top 50 clients now buy more than one solution, a sign of tightening client relationships and higher cross-sell potential.

Efficiency Programs Aim to Restore Margin Power

To address margin pressure and create room for continued investment, Concentrix is leaning heavily into automation and internal technology to drive structural cost takeout. The company reduced non-billable resources and infrastructure expenses by close to a $100 million run-rate exiting the first quarter of 2026. These efforts are intended to strip out duplicate and overcapacity costs that arose from previous growth and acquisitions. Management expects these efficiency gains to translate into sequential margin improvement throughout 2026, with the benefits layering on top of revenue mix improvements and AI-led productivity gains.

Noncash Goodwill Impairment Masks Underlying Performance

One of the most headline-grabbing items in the quarter was a $1.52 billion noncash goodwill impairment, which drove a GAAP net loss despite solid underlying operations. Management made clear that the impairment was driven by the company’s trading range and broader market conditions rather than a deterioration in the business fundamentals. While this charge significantly impacts reported GAAP earnings and book equity, it does not affect cash flow, and the company urged investors to focus on cash generation and non-GAAP metrics to gauge operational health.

Guidance Highlights Modest Growth With Self-Inflicted Headwinds

While the company delivered a solid finish to the year, its forward revenue guidance is conservative, reflecting both macro realities and deliberate strategic choices. For fiscal 2026, Concentrix expects reported revenue between $10.035 billion and $10.18 billion, equating to 1.5% to 3% constant-currency growth and about a 60 basis point FX tailwind. Management flagged an approximate 1% revenue headwind from further reductions in low-complex work and about 2% from actively “resolutioning” and optimizing client programs—an aggregate 3% drag that is largely self-imposed to upgrade the quality and profitability profile of the business. This means that underlying, like-for-like demand is somewhat stronger than the headline numbers suggest.

Margin Recovery Path and Near-Term Cash Timing Dynamics

Despite acknowledging year-over-year margin pressure—full-year non-GAAP operating margin of 12.8% remains below historical levels—Concentrix laid out a clear path to improvement. On top of the $100 million run-rate cost reductions and mix shift away from low-margin work, the company is absorbing short-term implementation costs tied to onshore-to-offshore migrations and transformational programs, which create temporary margin compression as duplicate capacity runs in parallel. Management expects these transition costs to fade, with sequential quarterly operating income improvement and a back-half margin recovery in 2026. Investors should also note that cash timing creates a seasonal dip: Q1 adjusted free cash flow is expected to be slightly negative, though better than last year, before rebounding for the full year.

Leverage and Sector-Specific Softness Remain Watchpoints

The balance sheet remains a central investor focus. With total debt at $4.639 billion and net debt of $4.311 billion, leverage is still high even after recent pay-downs. Management reiterated its commitment to ongoing deleveraging, supported by rising free cash flow and disciplined capital allocation. On the demand side, the company flagged some vertical-specific softness and competitive intensity: technology and consumer electronics, as well as healthcare, saw revenue decline about 2% in the quarter, partly driven by shore mix changes and volume trends. Pricing in more commoditized customer experience services remains “very, very competitive,” putting a premium on differentiation via technology, scale and complex solutions.

Guidance and Outlook: Slow Top Line, Improving Economics

For full-year 2026, Concentrix guided to reported revenue of $10.035–$10.18 billion, representing 1.5%–3% constant-currency growth and including an estimated 60 basis points of FX tailwind, alongside roughly 3% revenue headwinds from proactively reducing non-complex work and optimizing client programs. Non-GAAP operating income is forecast in the $1.24–$1.29 billion range and non-GAAP EPS between $11.48 and $12.07, based on continuing interest expense, a diluted share count of roughly 60 million and a 25% tax rate. Adjusted free cash flow is expected to climb to $630–$650 million, reinforcing the free-cash-flow-focused investment case. For the first quarter, the company projects revenue of $2.475–$2.5 billion with 1.5%–2.5% constant-currency growth, non-GAAP operating income of $290–$300 million and EPS of $2.57–$2.69, with slightly negative but improving free cash flow. Management signaled sequential quarterly operating income improvement, a stronger margin profile in the back half, CapEx at 2%–2.5% of revenue, continued targeted go-to-market spending and share repurchases roughly in line with fiscal 2025, all while maintaining a focus on debt reduction and a longer-term aspiration for mid-single-digit revenue growth.

In summary, Concentrix’s earnings call painted a picture of a company in the midst of a strategic reset: trading near-term GAAP optics and top-line acceleration for higher-quality revenue, stronger margins and more durable cash generation. Record adjusted free cash flow, improving non-GAAP margins and fast-growing AI-driven offerings underpin a constructive medium-term story, even as the massive goodwill impairment, elevated leverage, competitive pricing and modest growth guidance temper near-term enthusiasm. For investors, the key questions will be whether management can execute on its margin recovery plan, sustain cash flow growth and gradually unlock faster top-line expansion without overextending the balance sheet.

data.title: Top Clene Insider Quietly Snaps Up a Big Stake in Bold Market Move
data.description:
New insider activity at Clene ( (CLNN) ) has taken place on January 13, 2026.

Director David Matlin has made a notable purchase of Clene stock, acquiring 33,333 shares in a recent transaction. The buy, valued at $216,664, signals a meaningful vote of confidence from company leadership and marks a significant insider investment in Clene.

Recent Updates on CLNN stock

In the last 24 hours, news on Clene (CLNN) has focused on two main developments: a sizable financing and progress toward potential FDA approval for its lead ALS drug, CNM-Au8. The company entered into a registered direct offering of over $28 million structured in three tranches (common shares plus Series A and B warrants), which is expected to extend its cash runway first into Q3 2026 and, if warrants are exercised, potentially through late 2026 and into early 2027, funding clinical, regulatory, and possible commercialization activities. The warrants are explicitly structured around key FDA milestones and future share-price performance, signaling that funding access is being tied to regulatory progress and market validation. Separately, the FDA granted Clene an in‑person Type C meeting this quarter to review biomarker data (notably reductions in NfL, GFAP, and IGFBP7) that support CNM-Au8’s potential as an ALS treatment and the use of NfL reduction as a surrogate endpoint for accelerated approval. Recent analyst commentary and AI-based assessments suggest that while these clinical and regulatory updates are positive and may underpin more optimistic long‑term scenarios (and thus influence price targets), expectations remain tempered by Clene’s weak financial profile, ongoing losses, and bearish technical signals, which weigh on overall ratings despite the promising biomarker and survival data.

Spark’s Take on CLNN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLNN is a Neutral.

Overall score is weighed down primarily by weak financial performance (low revenue scale, heavy losses/cash burn, and negative equity). Technicals also remain bearish with price below key moving averages and negative MACD. data.title: Top Insider Just Snapped Up More Shares of This Real Estate Stock
data.description:
New insider activity at Agree Realty ( (ADC) ) has taken place on January 13, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Agree Realty’s Chief Financial Officer, Peter Coughenour, recently boosted his stake in the company by purchasing 500 shares of Agree Realty stock in a transaction valued at $34,900, signaling personal confidence in the company’s prospects.

Recent Updates on ADC stock

Over the last day, news on Agree Realty has centered on updated analyst views and detailed disclosures of its 2025 investment activity and balance-sheet position. RBC Capital trimmed its target after the company’s Q4 acquisition total came in slightly below that firm’s expectations, even though it still matched management’s guidance and cap rates were solid, suggesting only a modest recalibration rather than a change in fundamental view. Mizuho also reduced its target as part of a more cautious stance, while Evercore ISI’s small downward revision followed broader insights from the NAREIT conference, where the analyst saw shifting prospects across REIT sectors, with some segments facing ongoing challenges. This combination of robust growth metrics, strong balance-sheet liquidity, and slightly softer acquisition pacing versus certain forecasts is driving the recent fine-tuning of analyst price assumptions rather than wholesale changes in their broader outlook on the stock. Over the last 24 hours, Alumis Inc. has been in focus after positive Phase 3 results for its lead drug envudeucitinib in moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, which several analysts say make the data look competitive and materially reduce regulatory approval risk while expanding the drug’s long‑term revenue potential, prompting multiple upward revisions to their price expectations. H.C. Wainwright highlighted that the Phase 3 readout reset expectations for the oral TYK2 inhibitor landscape, citing both lower approval risk and a higher revenue ceiling as key drivers behind its more optimistic long‑term outlook. Morgan Stanley similarly raised its expectations after boosting its estimated probability of success for the psoriasis indication, reflecting stronger confidence in future cash flows from envudeucitinib. In parallel, Alumis launched and upsized a common stock offering—ultimately structuring a 17.65M‑share underwriting at $17 per share with an option for additional shares, increasing the total deal size from $175M to roughly $300M–$345M—to bolster its balance sheet and financial flexibility following the trial success. While the offering introduces dilution that can pressure shares in the near term, the company’s enhanced capital position, combined with the de‑risking of its late‑stage asset, underpins the more constructive analyst assumptions embedded in the recent target updates, even as AI-based analysis flags ongoing concerns around heavy cash burn, negative profitability, and legal risk. Recent trading has been active, with the stock previously more than doubling on the psoriasis data, and technical indicators currently skewing positive despite some near‑term consolidation around the secondary pricing level.
